name: Telephone Exchange System
tagline: Switched analogue speech networks — circuit establishment, ringing, billing, and human-scale switching fabrics
problem:
A telegraph excels at one-to-many urgency but feels rude for intimate coordination; everyone on a party line hears your negotiation with the midwife. Telephony wants switched circuits — a temporary electrical path between two voices — then later packetised speech, but the human problem remains: who may connect when, and who pays.

Manual exchanges trained operators to memorise personalities and faults; automatic exchanges replaced empathy with relays and then digital switches. Common-battery systems centralised power so subscribers did not each maintain a cellar full of wet cells.

Scaling introduced mathematics: blocking probabilities, trunk groups, and overload days when everyone picks up at once. Rural long loops demanded careful impedance design so speech stayed intelligible.

Today’s mobile cores hide complexity behind glass, but the exchange problem persists as routing tables, lawful intercept interfaces, and emergency services location — sociology wearing copper headphones.
overview:
Strowger-class stepping switches translated dial pulses into physical positions of wiper arms; crossbar systems separated control from paths; digital switches time-multiplex PCM samples through non-blocking fabrics. Outside plant moved from open wires to twisted pairs that reduced crosstalk; loading coils spaced inductance to extend voice band on long loops.

Signalling evolved: loop start, ground start, multifrequency tones, and common-channel signalling carried setup messages out-of-band. SS7-linked worlds enabled mobility and fraud simultaneously — every convenience is a new seam.

Powering remote terminals, cooling huts, and battery backup for blackouts are part of the voice promise. Fibre backhaul eventually swallowed copper trunks, but the last mile lesson remains: impedance, balance, and corrosion control.

Exchanges are also buildings with human rituals: shift logs, spare cards, and the coffee machine that kept 3 a.m. reroutes civil.

principles.name: Twisted pair balance
principles.explanation: Equal and opposite currents cancel radiated pickup — why untwisted house wire hums on audio.

principles.name: 4 kHz channel spacing
principles.explanation: Legacy FDM stacks 300–3400 Hz voice channels — defines digital sampling at 8 kHz after Nyquist guard.

principles.name: Blocking probability
principles.explanation: Erlang models size trunk groups — insufficient trunks yield post-dial busy flashes.

principles.name: Signalling separation
principles.explanation: Out-of-band SS7 decouples call setup from voice path — enables mobility and fraud if not authenticated.

components.name: Switching fabric
components.madeFrom: Electromechanical selectors or digital TDM buses

components.name: Line circuit card
components.function: Battery feed, overvoltage protection, ringing relay

rawMaterials.name: PE-insulated copper pairs
rawMaterials.spaceAlternatives: PE-insulated twisted copper pairs from cable plants; ship pre-made cable until extruders exist off-world — then oversize conductors slightly for colder nights and UV
rawMaterials.processingRequired: Colour code pairs; fill gel against moisture

rawMaterials.name: Central office steel
rawMaterials.spaceAlternatives: Galvanised steel frames from rolling mills; aluminium racks where weight matters, with bimetallic awareness at copper joints

rawMaterials.name: Rare metals in relays
rawMaterials.spaceAlternatives: Silver-cadmium-oxide relay contacts historically; solid-state relays and digital line cards where semiconductor supply is healthy

buildSteps.title: Model how many simultaneous conversations your town truly needs
buildSteps.description: Count phones, peak hour patterns, and emergency surge assumptions. Decide blocking target — not every village needs zero blocking if economics forbids it — but document the trade-off honestly.
buildSteps.warningNote: Underestimating Mothers’ Day traffic is a classic bruise

buildSteps.title: Engineer outside plant: gauge, twist, and length
buildSteps.description: Measure loop resistance and capacitance; place loading coils per tables if using long rural pairs. Colour-code pairs obsessively; photograph as-built before trenches refill.

buildSteps.title: Install MDF and protector blocks before subscriber prem
buildSteps.description: Bring pairs to a main distribution frame; terminate on protectors that shunt lightning. Label every jumper with origin and date — future you will bless the discipline.
buildSteps.warningNote: Shared shells can cross-couple noise if shields are mishandled

buildSteps.title: Stage switch or softswitch with recorded test calls
buildSteps.description: If using electromechanical heritage, oil pivots per manual; if digital, verify clocking and echo cancellation. Run load tests with artificial traffic before inviting the mayor.
buildSteps.warningNote: Clock drift causes slips and chirps

buildSteps.title: Write runbooks for outage, lawful intercept, and migration
buildSteps.description: Document how to reroute trunks, how to patch around card faults, and how to roll back software. Train two people per skill — buses still hit teams.
buildSteps.warningNote: Single-human knowledge is a single point of failure

history.event: Bell patents telephone; first exchanges follow within years.
Automatic Strowger switch reduces operator labour for small cities.

inventors.name: Alexander Graham Bell
inventors.contribution: Patent for harmonic telegraph leading to telephone

impact:
Telephony put voices in real time across cities — reshaping business, intimacy, and emergency response. Exchanges were the first large-scale connection-oriented networks computers later mimicked.

Recovery must preserve craft knowledge of copper plant, not only VoIP overlays.

videos.title: PSTN architecture overview
videos.description: Telecommunications engineering lectures on CO functions.
